pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'True Airspeed (TAS): 135 knots' (line 5 of task.json)
Task description explicitly states 'Fuel Burn: 14.5 GPH' (line 5 of task.json)

Flight planning calculation: Distance KMOD-KFAT = 78.0 NM (verified via great-circle formula using real airport coordinates from AirNav). ETE = Distance / TAS = 78.0 / 135 = 0.578 hours = 34.7 minutes. Value fits within claimed range of 33-40 minutes.
Same calculation as min_ete. Calculated value of 34.7 minutes falls within the claimed range of 33-40 minutes.
Fuel calculation: ETE = 0.578 hours, Fuel Burn = 14.5 GPH. Fuel = 0.578 × 14.5 = 8.38 gallons. Value fits within claimed range of 7.5-10.0 gallons.
Same calculation as min_fuel. Calculated value of 8.38 gallons falls within the claimed range of 7.5-10.0 gallons.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real aviation data with actual California airports KMOD (Modesto City-County) and KFAT (Fresno Yosemite International). The 78.0 NM distance between these airports has been verified using great-circle distance calculations with real coordinates from aviation databases (AirNav). With the specified TAS of 135 knots and fuel burn of 14.5 GPH, the calculated flight parameters are: ETE = 34.7 minutes (within metadata range 33-40) and Fuel = 8.38 gallons (within metadata range 7.5-10.0). All metadata claims are consistent with real aviation flight planning calculations. The task requires using the Avare aviation GPS/EFB app to set aircraft performance parameters and create a flight plan.
